name: Lock-in Period
text: The Parties agree that this Agreement shall not be terminable by either Party for a period of eleven (11) months from the Commencement Date ('Lock-in Period'), except in cases of material breach. During the Lock-in Period: (a) the Tenant shall not vacate the Premises before the expiry of the Lock-in Period and shall remain liable for rent for the entire Lock-in Period even if the Premises are vacated early; (b) the Landlord shall not seek possession of the Premises except in cases of non-payment of rent or material damage to the Premises; and (c) neither Party shall terminate this Agreement for convenience. In the event the Tenant vacates the Premises prior to the expiry of the Lock-in Period without the Landlord's written consent, the Tenant shall be liable to pay the Landlord rent for the remaining Lock-in Period as liquidated damages, which the Parties agree represents a genuine pre-estimate of the Landlord's loss.
invalid_if: Lock-in period penalty not a genuine pre-estimate of loss under ICA S.74
Lock-in period exceeds 11 months without registration under Registration Act 1908
source: Transfer of Property Act, 1882; Indian Contract Act, 1872
